heading: Multiple Types Example
body:
The following index definition example uses the
sample\_mflix.movies collection to index a field as multiple types. The
following index definition indexes the `title` field as the following
types:

- `autocomplete` type to support autocompletion for queries using
  the [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator.

- `string` type to support text search using operators such
  [text](text-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), and so on.

Visual Editor

- In the Add Field Mapping window, select
  title from the Field
  Name dropdown.

- Click the Data Type dropdown and select
  Autocomplete.

- Modify the Autocomplete Properties to set the
  following:

  - Analyzer to `lucene.standard`.

  - Tokenization to `edgeGram`.

  - Min Grams to `2`.

  - Max Grams to `15`.

  - Fold Diacritics to `false`.

- Click Add.

- Add another field mapping for the same field with
  Data Type set to String.

- Click Add.

JSON Editor

Replace the default index definition with the following
index definition.

```json
{
  "mappings": {
    "dynamic": true,
    "fields": {
      "title": [{
        "type": "autocomplete",
        "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
        "tokenization": "edgeGram",
        "minGrams": 2,
        "maxGrams": 15,
        "foldDiacritics": false
      },
      {
        "type": "string"
      }]
    }
  }
}
```
